subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF, World Bank, AfDB, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem) + Senegalese/French-language and international press; the CFA franc is pegged to the euro.

A strong democratic tradition that passed a 2024 turnover test, now grappling with a confirmed hidden-debt shock (the IMF program frozen) and fresh 2026 governing-coalition friction; new oil/gas is coming online.

Standout features: democratic resilience, new hydrocarbons, and low peg-anchored inflation, against a major hidden-debt revelation and data-integrity hit. Source-lean: LOW-MODERATE Anglophone (French press corroborated).

economic.notes: New oil/gas and low peg-anchored inflation, against a major hidden-debt shock that froze the IMF program.

fiscal_state.notes: A 2024 audit confirmed misreporting - IMF-consolidated debt ~132% of GDP (versus ~70% reported) and a 13.4% 2024 deficit - freezing the IMF program; consolidating but with no new program yet. (IMF Nov-2025; Court of Auditors)

monetary_independence.notes: The CFA-franc euro peg delivers stability and credibility but zero autonomy, France-guaranteed and regionally pooled. (BCEAO/IMF)

banking_stability.notes: A sound regional system, with some sovereign-exposure strain from the debt shock. (IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: New oil and LNG online from 2024-25 (boosting growth ~8%), plus gold/phosphates/fishing; a nascent-resource-curse risk and debt overhang temper. (Ecofin; IMF)

institutional.notes: A strong democratic tradition pursuing accountability, with the hidden-debt scandal a data-integrity hit.

rule_of_law.notes: A strong democratic tradition with a 2023-24 crackdown legacy; the new government pursues audits/accountability. (Freedom House; WJP)

statistical_integrity.notes: The hidden-debt scandal badly hurt credibility; corrective publications are improving but capacity is weak. (IMF; Court of Auditors)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Decent for the region and reforming, with a debt-management gap. (World Bank/IMF)

press_freedom.notes: Relatively free, with the RSF rank rising, against some online-harassment and lese-majeste jailings. (RSF; CIVICUS)

political_social.notes: A democratic turnover that passed a crisis test, with fresh 2026 coalition friction.

political_stability.notes: The 2024 turnover passed a near-constitutional-crisis test on a reformist mandate, but fresh 2026 governing-coalition friction adds near-term instability. (Al Jazeera; Africanews)

civil_liberties.notes: Improving post-2024 on a strong tradition, with residual speech-prosecution risk. (Freedom House)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: A regional hub with lawful-ish routes. (observed)

social_cohesion.notes: Easing Casamance separatism but youth-unemployment/emigration pressure, with the coalition rift a strain. (ICG)

demographics.notes: Very young and fast-growing - a dividend with a jobs/migration strain. (UN)

geopolitical.notes: A Western-aligned anchor recalibrating toward sovereignty, with Sahel-spillover risk.

alliance_reliability.notes: A Western/France-aligned anchor recalibrating (a full French-base withdrawal, a sovereigntist tilt) - not a junta pivot, net positive. (Euronews; France 24)

conflict_involvement.notes: No war, with Sahel-jihadist border-spillover risk and easing Casamance separatism. (ICG)

physical_practical.notes: Relatively good regional infrastructure, against Sahel drought, coastal erosion, and weak healthcare.

climate.notes: Sahel drought plus low-lying Dakar/Saint-Louis coastal erosion and sea-level, moderate readiness. (ND-GAIN; IPCC AR6)

healthcare.notes: Improving but weak, with low spend. (WHO)

infrastructure.notes: Relatively good and improving regionally - the capital, a new city, an airport, and a new deep-water port. (trade.gov)

crime_safety.notes: Relatively safe regionally, with urban street crime. (US State Dept)

summary: One of West Africa's strongest democratic traditions, which passed a 2024 turnover test under near-crisis conditions and is now bringing new oil and LNG online with low CFA-franc/euro-peg-anchored inflation and relatively good regional infrastructure. The dominant near-term shock is fiscal: a 2024 audit revealed hidden debt far above what had been reported (IMF-consolidated debt ~132% of GDP), freezing the IMF program and badly denting data integrity, atop fresh 2026 governing-coalition friction, Sahel-spillover risk, and severe coastal-erosion/drought exposure. The euro peg trades monetary autonomy for stability.
